message:
Storage/UI: add read-only database access for TUI detail view

Introduce a read-only database connection mode for the TUI to fetch
conversation details more efficiently without risking write conflicts
with the background indexer.

**src/storage/sqlite.rs**
- Add open_readonly() method that opens SQLite with:
  - SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY flag (prevents accidental writes)
  - SQLITE_OPEN_NO_MUTEX flag (no need for write mutex overhead)
- Apply common pragmas for performance consistency

**src/ui/data.rs**
- Change load_conversation() signature to accept &SqliteStorage reference
  instead of &Path
- Removes redundant connection opening on each detail view load
- Caller now controls connection lifecycle and can reuse it

**src/ui/tui.rs**
- Open a single read-only DB connection at TUI startup (db_reader)
- Gracefully handle case where DB doesn't exist yet (first run)
- Pass db_reader reference to load_conversation() for detail fetching
- Eliminates repeated open/close cycles when browsing results

Benefits:
- Reduced syscall overhead when rapidly scrolling through results
- No risk of read-only TUI interfering with background indexer writes
- Cleaner separation of concerns: TUI reads, indexer writes
